sources: Avoma call 2026-01-21 (External - Allstate and Zycus Product Session)
metrics.narrative: Pain points are well quantified in the discovery/demo cycle (25 Jun 2025 CLM demo: manual Word-based redlining and homegrown supplier-risk tracking; 26 Jun 2025 P2P demo: non-PO invoices and OCR-to-contract matching gaps, contract compliance required above $20,000 spend). What is missing is an Allstate-specific dollarized business case tying these to a committed ROI figure; the 21 Jan 2026 onsite discussed AI/negotiation savings generically ('autonomous negotiation flow to drive tactical savings') but no Allstate-specific number is on record.

champion.narrative: Gaurav (Rakesh Desai) has functioned as the primary conduit for over a year — coordinating the Jan 2026 onsite, connecting internally on the ANA POC framework (2 Mar 2026), and relaying leadership discussion on POC/commercials (26 May 2026). But he has gone silent on the single most decision-relevant question (the SAP Ariba beta outcome) despite three direct rep follow-ups (8 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) and the last confirmed buyer-side reply (an inbound email) was 22 May 2026, 48 days ago as of today. The champion is real but currently unresponsive and at risk.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the explicit, named gate on this decision: Allstate is running a live 'SAP Ariba workshop/beta program' (Next Step, 26 May 2026) and the outcome has not been shared despite repeated asks. Coupa and Ivalua are logged on the Salesforce Competitors field but have no corroborating call evidence in the last 13 months — they read as background/dormant, not active threats.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is deep and specific across the evaluation: manual contract redlining and homegrown renewal tracking (25 Jun 2025, speaker note), non-PO invoices with no contract/PO tie-back for tax payments (26 Jun 2025), fragmented contingent-labor tracking via SharePoint outside Fieldglass (15 Jul 2025), and a stated need to 'consolidate its technology landscape' (15 Jul 2025, speaker note).

sources: living memory / prior sweep note on stale commercial proposal
paper_process.narrative: No commercial, legal, or contracting motion has begun. The only forcing function on record is the Ariba renewal clock: 'resigning ariba 12 months expires in 12 months' (Next Step, 21 Jan 2026), meaning Allstate's current Ariba term runs to roughly Jan 2027. No MSA/SOW/redline activity, and the commercial proposal itself is stale (reported by the rep in 3 separate emails as roughly a year old and unrefreshed).

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris — Executive Sponsor)
economic_buyer.narrative: Casey McDowell (VP, Global Enterprise Procurement) and Cheryl Harris (SVP Procurement) both carry Executive Sponsor contact roles in Salesforce, so the economic buyer is identified structurally. But neither has appeared on a single Avoma-captured call across the 13-month history, and direct engagement has been attempted, not achieved: Karson Keogh was 'working on setting up executive session with Casey in Chicago' (Next Step, 4 Feb 2026), then noted Mohit 'was unable to attend PP due to US leadership being in India' and asked again for 'an executive meeting w Casey/cheryl' (Next Step, 18 Feb 2026). No confirmation of that session completing appears anywhere after.

decision_process.narrative: The process is visible and structured: a formal RFP-style, multi-stakeholder evaluation (sourcing, IT/architecture via Zulfikar Jeevanjee, legal via Elliot Stultz/Travis Rogers, business via Ryan Smith/Christopher Paquette) running in parallel with an internal SAP Ariba renewal beta/workshop. Allstate itself set a decision window of '3/4 months' from the 21 Jan 2026 onsite (i.e., roughly Apr-May 2026); that window has lapsed with no new date communicated as of the most recent Next Step update (29 Jun 2026).

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria have been exhaustively explored across a formal multi-day evaluation: CLM/redlining and supplier onboarding (25 Jun 2025), sourcing/category management and RFx scoring process (23 Jun 2025), P2P/invoice-matching and catalog controls (26 Jun 2025), and VMS/contingent-labor integration with Fieldglass (15 Jul 2025). The 21 Jan 2026 onsite added SAP-to-Zycus migration criteria (data migration approach, lift-and-shift timeline, AI reference proof).

Passive executive sponsorship is a risk—without Casey/Cheryl buy-in, the deal can lose to SAP incumbent inertia or stall indefinitely.

Reference calls were requested January 2026 ('need to set up different references ASAP' per Next Step 2026-01-21) and committed on the January on-site (5 calls, 2 with AI-focused customers), but none have been scheduled as of June 2026. Allstate is awaiting peer proof that Zycus can migrate them off SAP and that AI adoption is real. Without references, the decision risk stays high and favors the SAP incumbent.

ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) POC was offered February 2026 and has not started. The POC is the primary vehicle to prove Zycus AI is production-ready vs SAP's beta-stage capabilities. Without executing it, Zycus's AI differentiation remains a roadmap promise, not a proven advantage.

north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled at Shortlisted: Allstate has gone quiet since 22 May 2026 (46 days ago) while running a parallel SAP Ariba beta, and the ANA POC plus AI references promised since Feb 2026 remain undelivered, leaving the 31 Jul 2026 close date unreachable on current pace.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (not Upside Key Deal) until the SAP Ariba beta outcome is known and the POC/reference/commercial gates clear; realistic close is Q4 2026 or later given the undiscussed 9-month implementation runway.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A deeply-qualified, long-running evaluation (13 months, four in-depth demo sessions plus a multi-day onsite) has stalled behind an unresolved incumbent renewal decision and a champion who has gone silent for 48 days. The depth of the discovery is real, but nothing has moved forward since the January 2026 onsite.

deal_risk.text: Close date of 31 Jul 2026 is 22 days out while the buyer is still mid-evaluation of an incumbent renewal beta, with no commercial, legal, or InfoSec activity begun — the date is not credible.
The 21 Jan 2026 onsite itself discussed a ~9-month lift-and-shift implementation timeline, which is structurally incompatible with the current close date even under a best-case immediate win.

win_position.text: Deep, multi-threaded qualification exists — a 3-day, 2025 demo cycle plus a January 2026 executive-level onsite in India and Chicago covered CLM, P2P, sourcing, VMS integration, and migration planning in detail.
The single biggest determinant of the outcome, the SAP Ariba beta result, is entirely outside Zycus's control and undisclosed as of 29 Jun 2026 despite three direct asks.
Neither mapped economic buyer (Casey McDowell, Cheryl Harris) has ever engaged directly on a call, despite asks dating to 4 Feb 2026 and 18 Feb 2026.
The champion, Gaurav, has been unresponsive for 48 days (last inbound buyer reply 22 May 2026) on the exact question that determines the deal's direction.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-initiated action has occurred since the 22 May 2026 email reply — three rep follow-ups since (8 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) have drawn no response.
The only Avoma-captured meeting in the entire history sits at 21 Jan 2026 (~5.5 months / ~169 days ago); every touchpoint since is a Next Step text update, not a buyer session.
The POC offered 25 Feb 2026 to prove Zycus's AI differentiation has still not started as of 29 Jun 2026 — four months of drift on the exact proof point needed to counter SAP's beta.
